title: ferray-io: no allow_pickle parameter — NumPy's load has security considerations
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's np.load has allow_pickle=False by default to prevent arbitrary code execution from untrusted .npy files containing pickled objects. ferray-io doesn't support pickle at all (Rust doesn't have pickle), so this isn't a security issue. However, NumPy .npy files can contain object arrays (dtype=object) which are pickled — ferray will fail to load these with an unsupported dtype error. Document that ferray-io does not support object arrays/pickle, which is a feature (security) not a bug.
